summary: Support teams spend significant time manually reading, prioritizing, routing, and responding to tickets.
SaaS, IT, e-commerce, and customer support teams handling medium-to-high ticket volume.
TriageIQ predicts priority, recommends a routing group, scores SLA risk, and drafts a first response for human review.
A high-priority detection model and an improved grouped routing model using ticket text.
The final workflow uses grouped routing categories plus keyword overrides for obvious access and billing issues.
Rule-based SLA risk scoring is used because optional SLA ML models did not show strong predictive signal.
AI outputs are recommendations. Support agents review priority, routing, and response drafts before action.
Faster first response, fewer missed urgent tickets, improved routing consistency, and reduced manual triage effort.
Some datasets are synthetic or anonymized, routing groups are broader than original queues, and production deployment would require real company data and monitoring.
